content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 14,
1992
Glen H.
Spain,
Esq.
P.O.
Box 205
Comptche,
CA
95427
RE:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1—92-159
Dear Mr.
Spain:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”) .1/
Since your questions are general in nature and you
have not named the ballot measure committees on whose behalf you
have requested this advice,
we can only provide the following
informal responses to your questions pursuant to Regulation
18329(c) (4) .2/
QUESTIONS
1.
Under what circumstances is a statewide initiative
committee controlled by a state proponent?
2.
Is a statewide initiative committee controlled when the
proponent also sits as a member of the committee’s Board of
Directors?
3.
Can a sponsored committee be controlled by a state
proponent?
CONCLUSIONS
1.
The proponent ontro1s the committee if he or she exerts
significant influence on the actions or decisions of the
committee.
1/ Governmetit Code Section 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114;
Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
e
Sacramento CA 9S804-0807 • (916) 322-S66C


Glen H. Spain,
Esq.
Page
2
2.
The proponent may control the committee as a member of
the committee’s Board of Directors if he or she is a voting member
or in any way influences the actions or decisions of the
committee.
3.
There is nothing in the Act that prohibits a sponsored
committee from being controlled by a state measure proponent.
FACTS
You are a proponent and serve as general legal counsel for
two statewide ballot initiatives.
As general legal counsel you
serve in a purely advisory capacity.
You are not an officer nor a
member of the Board of Directors of either committee.
You
indicate,
however, that the Directors of the Boards establish
policy for their respective committees, hire employees and manage
the affairs of the committees..
You are compensated as an
independent professional,
not as an employee of either entity.
ANALYSIS
Section 82016 defines controlled committee as follows:
“Controlled committee” means a committee which
is controlled directly or indirectly by a
candidate or state measure proponent or which
acts jointly with a candidate, controlled
committee or state measure proponent in
connection with the making of expenditures.
A candidate or state measure proponent controls
a committee if he, his agent or any other committee
he controls has a significant influence on the
actions or decisions of the committee.
An initiative committee will be a controlled committee if the
proponent exerts significant influence on the actions or decisions
of the committee.
Where a proponent is a voting member of a
committee’s leadership,
in your example a member of the Board of
Directors,
he or she is presumed to be a controlling factor since
he/she probably exercises significant influence on the actions or
decisions of the committee.
(See,
Ferguson Advice Letter
No. A
86-044,
copy enclosed.)


Glen H.
Spain,
Esq.
Page
3
Section 82048.7 specifically excludes candidate controlled
committees from the definition of sponsored committee.
However,
there is nothing in the statute that prohibits a state measure
proponent from controlling a sponsored committee.
The statute
provides in part that:
-
“Sponsored committee” means a committee, other
than a candidate controlled committee,
which
has one or more sponsors.
Any person,
except
a candidate or other individual,
may sponsor a
committee.
(Emphasis added.)
Section 82048.7.
If you have any questions regarding this letter, please
contact me at 916/322-5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Ma y Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant
Enclosure
